description_long: This is a classic submachine gun with a wooden stock and a cylindrical drum magazine, reminiscent of the Soviet PPSh-41. The receiver and barrel assembly are constructed from dark metal, with a prominent round drum magazine mounted beneath the action. The wooden stock is smooth and extends to the rear, providing a sturdy grip and shoulder support. The barrel is relatively short and features a simple front sight post, while the rear sight is mounted on top of the receiver. The trigger guard and trigger are clearly visible, and the overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for rugged urban environments.
description: A vintage submachine gun with a wooden stock, metal receiver, and a large drum magazine beneath the barrel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, wooden stock, and prominent drum magazine.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular drum magazine and the alignment of the barrel and sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden stock, metal receiver, and drum magazine from the left.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the stock and the back of the drum magazine.
